content: Install HEPA filters with MERV 13+ rating in all HVAC systems. Replace filters every 3 months or when pressure drop exceeds 1.5 inches. Regular maintenance ensures optimal filtration efficiency.
note: This solution has been proven to reduce PM2.5 levels by 30-50% in indoor environments. Initial cost is moderate but provides long-term benefits.

content: Implement natural ventilation strategies by installing operable windows and cross-ventilation systems. Schedule ventilation during low-pollution hours (typically early morning). Use CO2 monitors to determine optimal ventilation timing.
note: Cost-effective solution with minimal ongoing expenses. Most effective when combined with outdoor air quality monitoring.

content: Deploy portable air purifiers with HEPA and activated carbon filters in high-traffic areas. Recommended: 1 unit per 50-100 sq meters. Ensure CADR rating matches room size for optimal performance.
note: Quick implementation option suitable for immediate relief. Higher operational costs due to electricity consumption and filter replacements.

content: Establish a comprehensive air quality monitoring network using IoT sensors. Deploy sensors in classrooms, hallways, and outdoor areas. Implement real-time data collection and alert systems for proactive management.
note: Enables data-driven decision making and early intervention. Initial investment required but provides valuable long-term insights.

content: Create green walls and indoor plant installations. Plants like spider plants, peace lilies, and snake plants can help reduce VOCs. Place strategically in corridors and common areas.
note: Aesthetic and functional solution that also improves student well-being. Low maintenance requirements once established.

content: Develop and implement a student health monitoring program. Track respiratory symptoms, absenteeism patterns, and correlate with air quality data. Use findings to adjust interventions and protocols.
note: Provides evidence-based insights for continuous improvement. Requires careful data management and privacy considerations.

content: Upgrade HVAC systems with variable-speed drives and smart controls. Optimize air exchange rates based on occupancy and outdoor conditions. Implement demand-controlled ventilation strategies.
note: Advanced solution with significant energy savings potential. Requires professional installation and may need building modifications.

content: Establish emergency protocols for high-pollution days. Include: indoor activity modifications, mask distribution, reduced outdoor time, and communication plans with parents. Create clear AQI thresholds for action.
note: Essential protective measure for student safety. Low cost, high impact solution that can be implemented immediately.

content: Conduct air quality audits and source identification studies. Identify specific pollution sources (indoor and outdoor). Develop targeted reduction strategies based on audit findings.
note: Foundation for effective air quality management. Helps prioritize interventions and allocate resources efficiently.

content: Implement source control measures: use low-VOC materials, maintain proper storage of chemicals, ensure adequate exhaust ventilation in labs and kitchens. Reduce indoor pollution sources at the origin.
note: Prevention-focused approach that addresses root causes. Often more cost-effective than treating symptoms.
